key_insight: Transfer-learning plus a Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss substantially improves BiSeNetV2 seam segmentation robustness under reflective welding conditions without increasing model size or speed.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical lightweight perception upgrade for autonomous construction welding robots that must detect thin seams on shiny, poorly lit metal surfaces in real time.
method: Start from a BiSeNetV2 backbone and apply transfer learning for seam segmentation.
Train with a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to improve boundary quality and region overlap.
Keep the architecture unchanged to preserve FLOPs, parameter count, and inference speed.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 percentage points over the OHEM baseline while recovering 96.33% of severe zero-IoU failures.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown in the reported welding datasets and conditions, so robustness on different robots, sensors, and field environments still needs validation.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
